articles.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
articles.description:
YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves.
The likeness detection feature uses a selfie-style scan of a person's face to monitor YouTube for lookalikes. If there is a match, YouTube alerts the user; the person then has the option to request that YouTube remove the content. YouTube has said in the past that it has found the number of removal requests to be "very small."
YouTube began testing the feature with content creators, and then expanded it to government officials, politicians, journalists, and fina …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Windows 11 tests an adjustable taskbar and resizable Start menu
articles.description:
Microsoft's latest Windows 11 test will allow you to reposition the taskbar and change the size of the Start menu. The update, which is rolling out to Windows 11 Insiders in the Experimental channel, lets you place the taskbar on the bottom, top, left, or right side of the screen.
Microsoft first teased its movable taskbar in March as part of efforts to rebuild trust among users. You can adjust the alignment of the icons inside the taskbar, as well as open the Start menu drawer from wherever you placed it. Windows 11 Insiders can access a shorter taskbar, too, which could come in handy for devices with smaller displays. There's also an opti …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
articles.description:
ArXiv, a popular platform for preprint academic research, is taking a new step to attempt to reduce the volume of papers that include AI slop.
If a paper has "incontrovertible evidence that the authors did not check the results of LLM generation," such as hallucinated references or "meta-comments" left by an LLM, authors will be banned from ArXiv for a year, according to Thomas Dietterich, ArXiv's section chair of its computer science section. Future ArXiv submissions will also have to be accepted at "a reputable peer-reviewed venue."
Here's what he said on X:
Attention @arxiv authors: Our Code of Conduct states that by signing your name …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The Analogue 3D is finally getting save states
articles.description:
If you’re not staying on top of Analogue 3D updates, now’s the time to start. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge	

Analogue has released a new firmware update for its Analogue 3D console that adds the Memories save state feature. Memories, which was first introduced with its handheld Analogue Pocket, allows you to save at any moment and resume from that exact spot later on, rather than having to reach a specific point in a game to save your progress. The feature should also help reduce some headaches with saving game progress on the Analogue 3D as its Virtual Controller Pak option still isn't available when using original or wired N64 controllers.
Although the feature was announced when the company first shared all the details about its 4K Nintendo 64 i …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
articles.description:
OpenAI announced yet another reorganization Friday, consolidating certain areas and making company president Greg Brockman the official lead of all things product.
In a memo viewed by The Verge, Brockman wrote that since OpenAI's product strategy for this year is to go all-in on AI agents, the company is combining its products to "invest in a single agentic platform and to merge ChatGPT and Codex into one unified agentic experience for all."
To do this, the company is making a suite of org chart changes, although it's still operating under some of the same ones from last month. That's when AGI boss Fidji Simo went on medical leave and Open …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: One of Intel’s fastest desktop CPUs is $50 off
articles.description:
The Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus was billed by the company in late March as one of its “fastest gaming desktop processor ever.” That hype was evidently accurate, as benchmarks done by Gamers Nexus show the processor giving AMD’s pricier X3D cache-equipped Ryzen 9000-series CPUs a run for their money. The Ultra 7 270K Plus also outperforms the Core i9 14900K in many tasks, which has long been a preferred choice for gamers.
Thankfully, it won’t cost you a ton to buy the 270K Plus if you’re considering a PC upgrade or building one from scratch. You can get this CPU at an all-time low price of $279.99 (about $45 off the normal price) right now at Amazon. 
Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus processor

Where to Buy:

 $325.99 $279.99 at Amazon

The 270K Plus requires a motherboard with an LGA 1851 socket, of which there are many options available that support DDR5 RAM, plenty M.2 SSDs, and with support for Wi-Fi 7. It’s great to see prices come down on some PC components, though the savings are tougher to appreciate when costs on RAM are still much higher than they used to be. If you’re looking for a range of parts, Micro Center has component bundles that loop in discounted RAM for the best prices on the web.
One nice perk of getting the CPU is that, through July 31st, you can register your purchase with Intel to get a full download of the Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight game.

articles.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
articles.description:
YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves.
The likeness detection feature uses a selfie-style scan of a person's face to monitor YouTube for lookalikes. If there is a match, YouTube alerts the user; the person then has the option to request that YouTube remove the content. YouTube has said in the past that it has found the number of removal requests to be "very small."
YouTube began testing the feature with content creators, and then expanded it to government officials, politicians, journalists, and fina …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: New crash data highlights the slow progress of Tesla's robotaxis
articles.description: Two crashes since July 2025 involved robotaxis being driven by remote operators.

articles.title: Windows 11 tests an adjustable taskbar and resizable Start menu
articles.description:
Microsoft's latest Windows 11 test will allow you to reposition the taskbar and change the size of the Start menu. The update, which is rolling out to Windows 11 Insiders in the Experimental channel, lets you place the taskbar on the bottom, top, left, or right side of the screen.
Microsoft first teased its movable taskbar in March as part of efforts to rebuild trust among users. You can adjust the alignment of the icons inside the taskbar, as well as open the Start menu drawer from wherever you placed it. Windows 11 Insiders can access a shorter taskbar, too, which could come in handy for devices with smaller displays. There's also an opti …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The Best Outdoor Deals From the REI Anniversary Sale 2026
articles.description: It’s the best time of year to pick up all the outdoor gadgets, tents, sleeping bags, and other gear you’ll need for summer fun.

articles.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
articles.description:
ArXiv, a popular platform for preprint academic research, is taking a new step to attempt to reduce the volume of papers that include AI slop.
If a paper has "incontrovertible evidence that the authors did not check the results of LLM generation," such as hallucinated references or "meta-comments" left by an LLM, authors will be banned from ArXiv for a year, according to Thomas Dietterich, ArXiv's section chair of its computer science section. Future ArXiv submissions will also have to be accepted at "a reputable peer-reviewed venue."
Here's what he said on X:
Attention @arxiv authors: Our Code of Conduct states that by signing your name …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.description: Each year, some of the power solar could have produced is blocked by aerosols.

articles.description: Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has a knack for raising money. So far, his three startups have raised more than $12.3 billion.

articles.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
articles.description:
YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves.
The likeness detection feature uses a selfie-style scan of a person's face to monitor YouTube for lookalikes. If there is a match, YouTube alerts the user; the person then has the option to request that YouTube remove the content. YouTube has said in the past that it has found the number of removal requests to be "very small."
YouTube began testing the feature with content creators, and then expanded it to government officials, politicians, journalists, and fina …
Read the full story at The Verge.

topStories.title: California lawmakers are working on a bill to preserve access to online games
topStories.description: Game preservation group Stop Killing Games advised on the bill.

topStories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
topStories.description: Instead of running silent and deep, most satellites easily stand out against the blackness of space.

topStories.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths
topStories.description: WHO learned of potential cases May 5; US CDC said it just heard about it yesterday.

topStories.title: The Trump administration is planning a prayer event on the National Mall. All but one of the speakers is Christian
topStories.description: The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.

topStories.title: Harvey Weinstein's third sex crimes trial in New York ends in mistrial
topStories.description: It was the second time in a year a jury was unable to reach a verdict on a rape charge brought by Jessica Mann.

topStories.title: The Steyer campaign pays influencers. Their posts don't always make that clear
topStories.description: Tom Steyer's campaign appears to have relied on paid influencers more than any candidate for governor, according to the most recent campaign finance filings.

topStories.title: Report: Conditions at immigrant detention centers in California have worsened under Trump
topStories.description: California enacted a law during the first Trump administration requiring state oversight of immigrant detention facilities. This is the fifth report released by the California Department of Justice since 2019.

topStories.title: First test of CO2 removal with green sand finds no harm to marine life
topStories.description: Adding olivine to the ocean could remove CO2 from the atmosphere, and a pilot project in New York state found no signs of adverse effects on seafloor organisms

topStories.title: Can the NFL's Rooney Rule survive the DEI backlash?
topStories.description: Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.

topStories.title: Top takeaways from final governor's debate: Knives out for Becerra
topStories.description: Candidates running to replace termed-out Gov. Gavin Newsom repeatedly attacked surging Democrat Xavier Becerra during a testy debate in a remarkably unsettled contest less than three weeks before the June 2 primary.

topStories.title: For Trump in China, a tonal shift yields few results
topStories.description: Trump's deference to Xi was a striking display of an often commanding president adapting to a new power dynamic, an acknowledgment of China's rise and its emerging role in the world.

topStories.title: Supreme Court, over two dissents, upholds abortion pills sent by mail, for now
topStories.description: The court rejects an antiabortion challenge to mifepristone, an FDA-approved medication for ending early pregnancies.

dailyNews.title: Two chemicals in everyday California air may pose 'unacceptable' cancer risk, findings suggest
dailyNews.description: California health officials have released new scientific findings that suggest the health risks from exposure to two chemicals present in ambient air, acrolein and ethylene oxide, are worse than previously known.

dailyNews.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
dailyNews.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

dailyNews.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
dailyNews.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

dailyNews.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
dailyNews.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

dailyNews.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
dailyNews.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

dailyNews.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
dailyNews.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

dailyNews.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
dailyNews.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

dailyNews.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
dailyNews.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

dailyNews.title: Enbridge CEO on Canada Pipelines, Natural Gas Outlook
dailyNews.description: Enbridge President & CEO Greg Ebel discusses the agreement between Canada and the Alberta province about the carbon tax deal. He discusses the ramifications for future pipelines in the country and how it can affect North America at large going forward. He speaks with Katie Greifeld & Isabelle Lee on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: CBS News Radio flashback: 2003 Space Shuttle Columbia disaster
dailyNews.description: On Feb. 1, 2003, the Space Shuttle Columbia disintegrated upon reentering Earth's atmosphere, killing all seven crew members on board. Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage from that day.

dailyNews.title: Iraqi man charged with planning U.S. terror attack on synagogue, prosecutors say
dailyNews.description: An Iraqi national was arrested and charged with planning to carry out several terror attacks in the U.S., including at a New York City synagogue and other prominent Jewish centers in Los Angeles and Scottsdale, Arizona. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

dailyNews.title: Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow calls GOP rival a "formidable competitor"
dailyNews.description: The Democratic primary for the Michigan Senate race is heating up with less than three months to go before the election. Candidate and state Sen. Mallory McMorrow joins "The Takeout" to make her case for the upper chamber seat.

topStories.title: Trump hits dramatic milestone in massive departure from Biden border plan: 'What a difference'
topStories.description: The Trump administration marks one year of zero releases at the southern border, touting an end to Biden-era catch-and-release policies, DHS says.

topStories.title: Eric Trump plans to sue Jen Psaki, MS NOW for alleged 'blatant lies' about him joining father's China trip
topStories.description: Eric Trump said he plans to sue MS NOW and host Jen Psaki for claims she made about him joining his father, President Donald Trump, on his recent China trip.

topStories.title: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran is enough
topStories.description: The US president says Tehran has to show "real" commitment to removing nuclear fuel and stopping uranium enrichment.

topStories.title: Mahmoud Khalil calls for deportation to be halted in light of new evidence
topStories.description: Lawyers say new evidence suggests Trump admin 'engineered' outcome of case against pro-Palestine Columbia student.

topStories.title: With Possible Raúl Castro Indictment, U.S. Eyes Venezuela Playbook
topStories.description: Amid stepped-up surveillance flights, a visit of the C.I.A. director and an energy embargo, the White House is trying to increase pressure on Cuba.

topStories.title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
topStories.description: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

topStories.title: What did Trump and Xi discuss during the China summit?
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump concluded a two-day summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump concluded a two-day summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

dailyNews.title: Trump touted Palantir on Truth Social after buying the company's stock, records show
dailyNews.description: During the first three months of the year, Trump purchased between $630,000 and $247,008 worth of Palantir stock and sold at least $1.1 million.

dailyNews.title: President Trump reveals Xi Jinping's response to possibly releasing imprisoned Chinese critic Jimmy Lai
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump told Fox News he brought up Jimmy Lai's case with Chinese President Xi Jinping but did not feel optimistic about the response.

dailyNews.title: Trump admin launches 'summer surge' of feds to make DC safest city in America ahead of 250th anniversary
dailyNews.description: Federal law enforcement launches massive summer surge in D.C. ahead of July Fourth America 250 celebrations, citing a 26% drop in crime and nearly 13,000 arrests in one year.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s ‘Learning Curve’ on China Ends With Conciliation at Summit
dailyNews.description: The president has shifted the foundations of American policy toward China, throwing aside the adversarial approach of recent years.

dailyNews.title: Xi Pitches His Vision for Avoiding Conflict With the U.S.
dailyNews.description: Having fought the Trump administration to a draw, China’s Xi Jinping is proposing “constructive strategic stability,” aimed at drawing lines he thinks the U.S. should not cross.

dailyNews.title: For Xi’s Critics, Trump’s China Visit Is Fuel for Jokes They Can’t Tell
dailyNews.description: On Threads and other sites, liberal-minded Chinese accounts were mocking the proceedings and offering a rare window into opinions on Xi Jinping and his leadership style.

dailyNews.title: Michigan Battles Trump Over His Order to Keep an Old Coal Plant Running
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration broke the law, Michigan and others told a court, by declaring an “energy emergency” and forcing an aging coal-burning plant to keep operating.

dailyNews.title: Trump Loyalist Leads Administration’s Hunt for Fraud in Elections
dailyNews.description: The loyalist, Dan Bishop, put his fealty to the president on display when he pressed the F.B.I. to chase investigative leads it had already rejected.

dailyNews.title: Boeing says China committed to buy at least 200 aircraft after Trump-Xi summit
dailyNews.description: President Trump is heading back to Washington, D.C., after his Beijing summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The president touted "fantastic trade deals" made between the U.S. and China. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the details.

dailyNews.title: Rep. Ro Khanna says he's "concerned that Trump may be compromising" U.S. assurances to Taiwan
dailyNews.description: President Trump wrapped up a two-day summit in China with future arms sales to Taiwan in some doubt and scant details on future Chinese purchases of American jets and soybeans. Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California joins "The Takeout" with his reaction to the trip.

dailyNews.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe meets with Cuban officials in Havana
dailyNews.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with senior Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday, following months of President Trump flirting with taking military action against the island nation. CBS News chief foreign affairs correspondent and "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan reports.

dailyNews.title: Lawmakers press Trump for military support for Taiwan after Xi summit
dailyNews.description: President Trump told reporters he made "no commitment either way" to China's Xi Jinping regarding U.S. weapons sales to Taiwan.
